trim_reasons: Jokic carries a volatile REB tag and the T10 pick sits just below his raw_avg of 13.4, but non-iron-floor REB on a volatile player means single-game variance (foul trouble, pace, MIN rotations) can push him under 10 on any given night. The post_miss_hr bounce-back framing also implies he misses more than twice a season at this tier.

Murray carries a volatile 3PM tag against a tough 3PM defense with a post_miss_hr of only 62% (below-average bounce-back), and the historical lesson confirms 3PM props are the highest-variance type on any slate — a cold-shooting night against tough perimeter defense is a realistic miss scenario even with a generous raw_avg of 3.6.

Johnson is volatile against a tough NYK defense with a FG_COLD flag already noted, and the pick was stepped down from T20 to T15 due to H2H failures — the floor at T10 is contingent on him shooting adequately in a road game against one of the better defensive teams. The typical_miss of 3 pts below means a cold night lands him right at or under the number.

The post_miss_hr of 61% with a negative lift (0.82) is the lowest bounce-back figure on an AST pick in this slate, meaning Johnson's AST production is not reliably self-correcting. A road game against NYK's tough defense also limits Atlanta's offensive flow, which is the primary engine for Johnson's assist opportunities.

Alexander-Walker is volatile with a tough NYK defense, and the typical_miss of 4 pts below tier means a miss isn't a near-miss — he could fall well short. The pick was stepped down through a negative FG_MARGIN cascade and still landed at a raw_avg barely above the line (22.9), making shooting variance the dominant risk.

Volatile 3PM tag with a post_miss_hr of only 69% and lift below 1.0 — the historical lesson flags non-iron-floor 3PM on volatile players as the highest-risk prop type, and a road game against NYK's perimeter defense amplifies cold-shooting downside even with H2H support at T3.

Ingram's min_floor of 23.4 is structurally fragile for a PTS T10+ pick — one low-minutes game (game script blowout as heavy underdog at +8.5) puts the floor at genuine risk. The post_miss_hr of only 72% with negative lift (0.94) also signals this player does not consistently bounce back after misses.

Mitchell is volatile with BLOWOUT_RISK at -8.5 — even after the blowout penalty was applied, a large spread on CLE's side means Q4 rest risk is real if CLE builds a big lead early. The volatile tag plus a typical_miss of 4 pts below tier means the downside scenario isn't a near-miss, it's a significant shortfall.

BLOWOUT_RISK at -8.5 combined with volatile AST tag against a tough defense — if CLE blows out TOR early, Mitchell's garbage-time minutes compress and his assist opportunities dry up. Raw_avg of 5.0 with AST over 4 is a thin margin for a volatile player in a potential running-clock scenario.

Mobley has a min_floor of only 22.0 in a BLOWOUT_RISK game (-8.5 CLE favored) — if CLE runs away with it early, Mobley is the first big rotation player to see reduced fourth-quarter minutes. Combined with a volatile tag, FG_COLD flag, and concerning H2H at T15 (1/3) and T10 (1/3), this pick's floor is contingent on both adequate minutes AND adequate shooting, with structural risk on both dimensions.

The post_miss_hr of only 56% with deeply negative lift (0.66) is the weakest bounce-back figure on the entire slate — Mobley's AST production is not self-correcting and in a BLOWOUT_RISK game his involvement in half-court offense in the fourth quarter is not guaranteed.

BLOWOUT_RISK at -8.5 combined with FG_MARGIN_THIN means Harden needs both adequate field goal efficiency AND meaningful second-half minutes — two independent requirements both at risk in a blowout scenario. His min_floor of 27.7 is adequate but he's a veteran who has historically seen reduced fourth-quarter run when games are decided.

Volatile AST tag in a BLOWOUT_RISK game against a tough defense — the post_miss_hr of 60% with negative lift (0.78) means Harden's assist production is not bounce-back reliable, and a blowout game script actively compresses the late-game playmaking minutes that drive high-assist totals for secondary playmakers.

Edwards is QUESTIONABLE (knee) — while Pick applied the -5% penalty, a knee soft-tissue concern on a volatile scorer means the injury itself could suppress his aggressiveness attacking the rim, which is core to his scoring floor. The post_miss_hr of only 55% with deeply negative lift (0.67) signals this player's PTS distribution is genuinely wide on off nights.

Towns has a min_floor of only 19.8 — among the lowest on the slate for a REB pick — meaning a single foul-trouble game or game-script adjustment produces a miss. His raw_avg of 11.6 supports the number, but the floor is contingent on adequate minutes and his foul-trouble history makes this structurally fragile.

Durant is QUESTIONABLE (knee) — a soft-tissue knee concern on a volatile 37-year-old scorer means game-time availability and in-game performance suppression are both non-trivial risks, and even after the -5% injury penalty the volatile tag means the downside scenario is a material scoring shortfall, not a near-miss.
